# Example dataset

This directory contains a small example dataset for testing and reproducing the output of **Nucleoid Mito Classifier**.

Only small, anonymized, and redistributable test fixtures should be placed in this directory. Do not add patient-identifiable, confidential, or unpublished microscopy data without explicit permission.

## Files

* `example_input_publication.tif` — two-channel example input image.
* `example_output_reference.zip` — reference output generated by the plugin from the example input.

## Input image

The example input has:

* image size: 2048 × 2048 pixels;
* bit depth: 8-bit;
* exactly two channels;
* C1: nucleoid signal;
* C2: mitochondrial signal;
* one Z slice;
* one time point;
* spatial calibration in micrometres;
* pixel width: approximately 0.0901435 µm;
* pixel height: approximately 0.0901435 µm.

The TIFF file was cleaned of the saved ImageJ ROI and unnecessary metadata without changing the pixel values.

## Reference analysis

The reference output was generated using the JAR produced by a successful GitHub Actions build from the `main` branch after input validation was added.

The complete parameter record is included in:

```text
example_input_publication-Analysis_Log.txt
```

## Expected results

A successful reference run produces:

| Result                    |           Expected value |
| ------------------------- | -----------------------: |
| All C1 objects            |                      583 |
| Colocalized               |                      394 |
| Borderline                |                       47 |
| Out of mitochondria       |                      142 |
| Filtered                  |                        0 |
| Mitochondrial mask status |            `OK_STANDARD` |
| Mitochondrial mask method | `STANDARD_BG20_TRIANGLE` |

The expected per-object CSV contains:

* 583 data rows;
* 63 columns;
* object IDs from 1 to 583;
* exactly one classification for every detected object.

## Fixture documentation requirements

For every future test fixture, document:

* image dimensions and channel order;
* pixel calibration and unit;
* expected detected-object count;
* expected classification counts;
* expected mask QC status;
* provenance and permission for redistribution.

## Integrity verification

The reference ZIP contains `SHA256SUMS.txt`. After extracting the archive, verify the checksums on macOS or Linux from inside the extracted directory:

```bash
shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS.txt
```

## Data provenance, attribution, and licence

### Creator

Nikol Volfová

### Affiliations

1. Department of Paediatrics and Inherited Metabolic Disorders, First Faculty of Medicine, Charles University and General University Hospital in Prague, Prague, Czech Republic
2. Department of Biochemistry, Cell and Molecular Biology, Third Faculty of Medicine, Charles University, Prague, Czech Republic

### Data provenance

The example image was derived from a collaborative microscopy project carried out at the First Faculty of Medicine, Charles University.

The image was anonymized and cleaned of unnecessary metadata for use as a reproducible software test fixture. Permission to publicly redistribute this specific example image was confirmed by the project lead.

Department of Paediatrics and Inherited Metabolic Disorders, First Faculty of Medicine, Charles University and General University Hospital in Prague, Prague, Czech Republic; Department of Biochemistry, Cell and Molecular Biology, Third Faculty of Medicine, Charles University, Prague, Czech Republic; Department of Biochemistry, Cell and Molecular Biology, Third Faculty of Medicine, Charles University. 2026. CC BY 4.0.

This data licence applies only to the example input image and reference outputs stored in the `examples/` directory. The Nucleoid Mito Classifier software source code is distributed separately under the BSD 3-Clause License.

The example dataset is provided as a reproducible software test fixture. It is not presented as a biological benchmark or as validation across different microscopy systems, sample types, or acquisition conditions.
